text: We're between 140 and 150 BPM — maybe between like 130 and 150 — so we're at 143 here.
We're in D-sharp minor.
All throughout the track you're going to have these chords.
It's a D-sharp minor 9 — root, third, fourth, sixth, and then a ninth.

The same chord is being played on both pads. One's playing like a rhythm and then the other one's just playing a chord, but they're the same chord, and it kind of just creates this big kind of like ethereal wash.
Two sine waves, low pass with a nice envelope on it and an envelope like that.
A ton of Unison — voices all the way up.
Chorus, delay, Reverb, autopan to make it kind of bounce off the kick as if it's being side-chained.
High pass.
Two square waves, they're detuned. Low bass filter, LFO is moving it, no envelope, and a bit of key tracking plus automation.
AMP up like that, bunch of Unison, slow LFO on the filter.
Chorus, delay, Reverb, autopan, side chain, high pass.
They're panned a bit too, so it kind of spreads them a little and it helps the overall width of the track.
Just a nice fat lo-fi 909 techno-type kick.
The bassline is doing this every time and then just changing the note.
We're in D-sharp minor so it's like root note, third, seventh — sixth, excuse me — fifth, seventh.
There's an Amber wavetable and then a saw wave. The Amber isn't quite as loud but it adds a lot of fatness to the bass.
Low pass filter with a nice envelope on it. That same envelope is also on the wavetable position on the first oscillator.
Auto pan to make it bounce off the kick.
On the group there's a little bit of saturator. The dry/wet is down to maybe like 35%. It's not really trying to fix anything — all it's doing is it's gluing these a little bit more.
Just a nice 909 hat — made it a bit shorter and also pitched it down so it grooves really well with the bass and kick. You can even make it shorter and just dial that groove in.
There's a house loop which is just high-passed — getting some life and some atmosphere, and it kind of adds to that main hi-hat.
There are some breakbeats — mainly doing autopan to make it like side-chained, and then a high pass filter.
There's an amen — chopped a bit to fit. One more break on top of that.
A bit of saturator on the drum group as well — same story. This one is making it a bit fatter but it's really just gluing everything, kind of helping to turn it into one thing without destroying it.
It's that really repetitive rhythm — like with the bassline — and then just changing the note. It bounces off the bass: that contrast helps to create a lot more groove and rhythm in the track.
It's a little vocal stab — warped and stretched a lot, and then the transient was taken off: you can see a little bit of release.
Delay, Reverb, and an autopan to make it bounce off the kick side-chain style.
These are panned a little bit, which really helps to keep everything where it's meant to be and makes it feel more like call and response because they're really talking to each other.

Notes are really simple — if we're in D minor we're playing with fourth, third, ninth, and then a root down here. It's really only a few notes but it just creates these little kind of melodic things at the end of every two bars.
Sub oscillator, Transformations, and then another Transformations — both panned.
Filter moving around with LFOs — just trying to bring it to life and make it a bit more exciting.
Unison voices turned down all the way — you barely hear that Unison but it just gives it that kind of chunky sort of feel.
A lot of Reverb. Autopan to make it bounce off the kick. High pass.
Like sixth or fifth sixth, route.
It's a pretty basic patch — basically a super saw. Shimmer Unison voices all the way up. That also helps make it different from the other one because one's very Unison and then one's clearly not.
A bit of Reverb. A bit of autopan to make it bounce off the kick. High pass filter.
